source_pr.title: planner: fix wrong results using the unique index column with cached plans
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #29296

Problem Summary: planner: fix wrong results using the unique index column with cached plans

### What is changed and how it works?

planner: fix wrong results using the unique index column with cached plans

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
planner: fix wrong results using the unique index column with cached plans
```

symptoms: tk.MustExec(`prepare stmt from 'SELECT * FROM UK_MU14722 WHERE col2 > ? OR col2 BETWEEN ? AND ? ORDER BY COL2 + ? LIMIT 3'`) (planner/core/prepare_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustExec(`prepare stmt from 'SELECT * FROM UK_MU14722 WHERE col2 > ? OR col2 BETWEEN ? AND ? ORDER BY COL2 + ? LIMIT 3'`) (planner/core/prepare_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustExec(`prepare stmt from 'SELECT * FROM UK_MU14722 WHERE col2 > ?.
fix_pattern: tk.MustExec(`prepare stmt from 'SELECT * FROM UK_MU14722 WHERE col2 > ? OR col2 BETWEEN ? AND ? ORDER BY COL2 + ? LIMIT 3'`) (planner/core/prepare_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustExec(`prepare stmt from 'SELECT * FROM UK_MU14722 WHERE col2 > ? OR col2 BETWEEN ? AND ? ORDER BY COL2 + ? LIMIT 3'`) (planner/core/prepare_test.go)

analysis:

Triage summary: tk.MustExec(`prepare stmt from 'SELECT * FROM UK_MU14722 WHERE col2 > ? OR col2 BETWEEN ? AND ? ORDER BY COL2 + ? LIMIT 3'`) (planner/core/prepare_test.go)
Changed files (3): executor/explainfor_test.go, planner/core/common_plans.go, planner/core/prepare_test.go
Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
